name: Sirius
description: The brightest star in the night sky, located in Canis Major.

name: Canopus
description: Second-brightest star, found in the constellation Carina.

name: Arcturus
description: Brightest star in Boötes and fourth-brightest in the night sky.

name: Vega
description: Part of the Summer Triangle, in the constellation Lyra.

name: Capella
description: Brightest star in Auriga, sixth-brightest in the night sky.

name: Rigel
description: Blue supergiant in Orion, representing the hunter's left foot.

name: Procyon
description: Brightest star in Canis Minor and eighth-brightest overall.

name: Achernar
description: Brightest star in Eridanus, known for its extremely rapid rotation.

name: Betelgeuse
description: Red supergiant in Orion marking the hunter's right shoulder.

name: Hadar
description: Second-brightest star in Centaurus.

name: Altair
description: Brightest star in Aquila and part of the Summer Triangle.

name: Acrux
description: Brightest star in the Southern Cross (Crux).

name: Aldebaran
description: Brightest star in Taurus, the 'eye of the bull'.

name: Spica
description: Brightest star in Virgo; also known as Alpha Virginis.

name: Antares
description: Red supergiant in Scorpius, rival of Mars in colour.

name: Pollux
description: Brightest star in Gemini and the nearest giant star to the Sun.

name: Fomalhaut
description: Brightest star in Piscis Austrinus, surrounded by a dusty disc.

name: Deneb
description: Brightest star in Cygnus and part of the Summer Triangle.

name: Mimosa
description: Second-brightest star in the Southern Cross (Crux).

name: Regulus
description: Brightest star in Leo, also called the 'little king'.
